title: Day 28: Day 28 — Handling Rejection
content:
# Day 28 — Handling Rejection

## Topic
Staying effective when prospects say no, ghost you, or choose to stay put

## 3–5 Key Points
- **Rejection is normal:** In recruiting, no’s, delays, and non-responses are part of the job, not proof that the strategy is broken.
- **Track what happened:** Patterns in objections, ghosting, or failed closes can show where your pitch or process needs work.
- **Don’t get emotional or needy:** The fastest way to look weak is to chase people with bruised-ego energy.
- **Use rejection to improve your approach:** Better targeting, sharper messaging, and stronger follow-up usually beat working harder in the same broken way.
- **Stay resilient and keep moving:** Good recruiters do not overreact to one lost candidate; they protect consistency.

## Why It Matters
If you can’t handle rejection, you won’t recruit at a high level for long. The people who win are the ones who stay steady, learn from losses, and keep the pipeline moving without turning bitter or desperate.
